When the quality of the products changes, the QuID  is updated and a row is added to this table. The 
third column specifies which sections or sub -sections have been updated. The fourth column should 
mention the version of the product to which the change applies.

KEY DEFINITIONS 
Geostrophic current: The current resulting from the balance between  the horizontal pressure gradient 
and the Coriolis force. 
Low pass boxcar filter: A filter applying the boxcar function, which is zero over the entire real line except 
for a single interval where it is equal to a constant > 0. 
Mixed layer depth: The depth at which the density has increased by a magnitude of 0.03 kg/m3 from its 
value at a depth of 10m. This represents the layer which is close to being homogeneous due to mixing 
by surface fluxes, notably the wind stress. 
z-isopycnic surfaces: A hybrid set of surfaces based on distance to the surface at rest (z) and surfaces 
where the density is constant (isopycnic). 
Isopotential surfaces : Surfaces along which there is no component of the gravitational force, i.e., 
surfaces with the same geopotential. Note that this is (nearly) identical to standard z surfaces.
